name: Chili Crusted Steak Fajitas
recipeIngredient: 1 medium red bell pepper, quartered and seeded
1 medium green bell pepper, quartered and seeded
1 medium yellow bell pepper, quartered and seeded
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat from the steak and set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine quartered bell peppers, tomato halves, 2 tablespoons lime juice, Worcestershire sauce, 3/4 teaspoon salt, and 1 clove minced garlic. Toss gently and set aside.
3. Place remaining ingredients (marinade components) into a large zip-top plastic bag: the remaining 1 tablespoon lime juice, Worcestershire sauce, 3/4 teaspoon salt, the remaining garlic cloves, and flank steak. Seal the bag and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. Remove the steak from the bag, reserving the marinade.
4. Rub the chili powder evenly over the steak. Set aside.
5. Place the bell pepper pieces, tomato halves, and onion slices on a grill rack coated with cooking spray. Grill the tomatoes for 3 minutes per side. Grill the steak for five minutes per side or until desired doneness is reached, basting occasionally with reserved marinade.
6. Grill the bell pepper pieces and onions for six minutes per side or until tender, basting occasionally with reserved marinade.
7. Remove the steak and vegetables from the grill. Let the steak stand for five minutes before slicing it diagonally across the grain into thin slices. Slice the bell peppers and onion in half.
8. Sprinkle remaining 3/4 teaspoon salt over the vegetables.
9. Heat flour tortillas according to package directions. Place two tortillas on each of four plates. Divide the steak, bell pepper, and onion evenly among the tortillas. Top each fajita with half a tomato and 1/4 tablespoon fat-free sour cream. Serve warm.
